title: The Macro Minute: Is the stock market about to bubble?

Happy Wednesday out there, team 42. It's
your skipper here, Darius Dell to
present our macro minute for Wednesday,
September 24th, 2025. As always, we'll
start with the executive summary from
today's leadoff morning note. If you
would like to review the analysis
supporting these conclusions and to see
if you need to make any changes in your
portfolio to remain on the right side of
market risk or to get on the right side
of market risk to the extent you haven't
been following KISS or Dr. Mo, need to
be a client of 42 macro. So today's key
macro question is, is the stock market
about to bubble?
Our answer perhaps. We certainly
wouldn't rule out this increasingly
probable outcome. The S&P 500 has surged
34% and added nearly 16 trillion dollars
in market value since early April, going
107 sessions without a 2% drop, the
longest streak since July 2024 and more
than 5 months without back-to-back 1%
declines. Despite consensus's myopic
focus on lingering trade policy
uncertainty, a UBS basket of
unprofitable tech companies has jumped
21% since the end of July, outperforming
their profitable tech baskets 2% gain
and the NASDAQ 100 6% advance, signaling
market euphoria continues to heat up.
For what it's worth, we told you to stop
focusing on the trees that tariffs
represent and start focusing on the
forest that is paradigm C when we
authored the theme back in April. We
hoped you listened.
If you didn't, that's okay, too. Today
is always the best day to begin
migrating your portfolio to the right
side of market risk. If we're still
right on paradigm C a year from now, US
stocks will likely be in full-blown
bubble territory by then. Bubbles always
end very poorly, but it's impossible to
time their conclusion based on
fundamental research alone. Even a
consensus top three investor of all
time, Stan Ducken Miller had to blow out
his entire shortbook and go on sbatical
in the late 1990s after losing $600
million in a few weeks, nearly double
that sum in today's dollars. While I
have no quantitative basis for
deciphering my left big toe from my
right elbow when it comes to gauging
what inning we might be in in the AI
super cycle, I do know that companies
panic buying capex equipment is more
likely late cycle than early cycle.
The problem with being a bare porn
purveyor is that late cycle is usually
where the right tail risk is most
extreme. Although I probably shouldn't
put this in writing, the missionoriented
investor in me actually hopes the stock
market bubbles so we can put bare porn
purveyors out of business once and for
all. Few things in our industry are
worse than scaring gullible retail
investors from maximizing upside capture
in bull markets because not
participating prevents them from
achieving their goal of an ontime and
comfortable retirement.
So, why do bare porn purveyors do it?
Because conjuring fear sells newsletter
subscriptions much, much faster than
helping investors confidently and
dispassionately maximize upside capture.
Something I've witnessed firsthand
throughout my career. Shame on anyone
and everyone who is consistently pumping
out Bearporn to generate clicks and
newsletter subscriptions at the expense
of their audienc's financial freedom.
Transitioning to our 42 micro dashboard
here. As always, we wrap up with a
question from our community. This one's
titled 40% gold bitcoin wins starting

love the awesome work on the kiss model

dollar and falling interest rates my

and bitcoin have run very high over the
years so buying now I'd be very late to
the game what is your thought on

has it run that hot and might have a big
tailwind due to stable coins. I'd assume

and implicitly lower the interest rates
of treasuries potentially even

thoughts uh with treasuries on Ethereum?
Uh I was mainly thinking due to the
yielding factor which is a plain

sure if I understand that. Anyways,
curious on your thoughts. Uh thanks.
appreciate you. So, uh, phenomenal
question and thanks for the kind
compliments, uh, via KISS. As you guys
all know, I use KISS to manage my entire
liquid net worth. We have thousands of

degrees. Some using it to manage their
entire retirement account, some using it
to manage a sliver of their brokerage
account in that entire spectrum, filled
in all the way in between. Uh and so and
and so in terms of answering this

uh any client question or you know just

particular investor. All of our research
is general in nature and is not you know
designed to you know communicate to one
individual or group of individuals uh as
an investor. So uh with that said just
kind of broadly thinking about uh the
market risk here and this is why I
picked this question to go to to pair

schemac question is is the stock market
about to bubble obviously if the stock

higher prices than cryptocurrencies like
bitcoin uh and ethereum as well. So it's
likely that we would see a uh a
continued upside bull run uh in those in
those types of securities. The one thing
I would say uh civically as it relates
to the question is
you know this concept of it's run a lot.
Therefore you're late is a very um in my
opinion a a misnomer. It's just not a
something you need to get rid of that
line of thinking from your uh investing
playbook. Like it just it is not doing
you any good to think like that as an
investor. Pull up a hundred-y year chart
of the stock market. Pull up a 15 20
year chart of Bitcoin. pull up a, you
know, however long chart of Ethereum
since, I think it came out in 2017. Pull
up a chart of Ethereum since 2017. Pull
up a chart of any risk asset over a
multi-year or multi-deade time horizon
and you're going to generally see that
it's straight up and to the right. And
so, you know, just kind of looking at
our Dr. Mo signal for um for Ethereum
specifically, you know, the green the
dark green represents the long max
position in terms of when Dr. Mo's
telling you to be long max position in
this particular asset class. The light
green tells you to be a long half
position. The gray tells you no position
and the red tells you to be uh um short
max position. And so as you can see in
this most recent Ethereum bull market in
terms of when Dr. Mo told you to buy
Ethereum back in uh April 30th, you
know, we've obviously rallied uh
tremendously more than doubled uh in
Ethereum um you know since uh since
since Dr. Mo said, "Hey, go get max loan
Ethereum." You know, you could have
challenged that signal here here
here up until today's price. And so you
know this is what we say. Hey, are you
on the right side of market risk? It's
not about where we came from. It's not
about where we're going, where you think
we're going. It's about what are the
market signals telling you where we're
likely headed. And again, it has nothing
to do with your emotional response to
trailing performance. It has nothing to
do with your fundamental research
process on where we're headed. It has
everything to do with what the other
investors, i.e. the market, think is the
most appropriate position to take. And
so, you know, the tools, the risk
management overlays that we built um
are, you know, based on proven
quantitative techniques across the
global buy side. And those proven
quantitative techniques help us to
dispassionately just say, "Hey, I don't
care about the past. I don't care about
the future. As long as Dr. Mo tells me I
need to be maxed on Ethereum today, I
need to be maxed on Ethereum today and
just check on the signal when it when it
changes and and respond as passionately
uh and confidently when it changes. You
know, obviously Dr. Mo has a phenomenal
history of trading these b major bull
runs uh in Ethereum as evidenced by this
chart here. Same thing with gold. I
mean, I you know, I think the one of the
worst things you can do as an investor
right now is pull up a chart of gold and
say, "Hey, you know, it's over. It's
over. It's over. It's over." Again, dark
green equals Dr. Gold tells you to be
the next long gold. And so, it's not
over. Is it over? Is it over? Is it
over? Is it over? Is it over? It is
over. A bunch of people thought it was
over for the past few months when it was
consolidating and then boom, it's
supposed to move higher. And so, you
know, this is the point we're making.
It's that you don't have to use, in
fact, we would caution against using
fundamental predictions to manage risk
in your portfolio. Um, you can use
fundamental predictions to decide which
factor bets you take. But you guys all
know that by and large, I don't think
retail investors should be taking factor
risk. You have the unenviable, highly
enviable competitive advantage relative
to institutional investors to be able to
just collect market beta. You can get
the return of gold. You can get the
return of Ethereum. You can get the
return of the stock market. You don't
have to pick small caps versus large
caps. You don't have to pick high beta
versus low beta. You don't have to pick
international versus US. you can just go
belong the US market, the global market
and just collect global equity beta or
US equity beta, you know, from that
perspective. And so, um, you know, same
thing with gold here. You know, you
don't have to go pick the, uh, the most
profitable or the least profitable gold
miner. Um, what you can do is you just
go collect the return and bullion. I
think gold is up, you know, almost 100%.
It's almost doubled since Dr. Mo told
you get long back in October of 2023.
So, you know, you don't have to know
anything about the future to wake up and
know that Dr. Mo is still telling you to
be maximum gold. And so that's the whole
point of what we're trying to you know
what we built here and what we're trying
to communicate is that you know so much
of investing is you know stressful you
know did I miss the move you know is the
is about to reverse about that major
factor reversal particularly if you
think about buy side investors and so
much of that can be eliminated all that
additional stress can be easily
eliminated by relying on proven
quantitative you know risk management
overlays now you don't have to rely on
our proven quantitative risk management
overlays you know obvious are incredibly
powerful and you know kept thousands of
investors
on the right side of market risk, you
know, for almost a half decade now. Uh,
and you know, we don't expect to to to
lose our competitive advantage in that
regard. But even if you don't partner
with 42 macro, I highly recommend you
partner with someone that allows you to
eliminate the stress associated with
picking factors, worrying about if the
market's about to reverse, worrying if
you're too late to the trade, because at
the end of the day, all that stuff is
just emotional decision-making that is
clouding your judgment. when the reality
is investing should be a emotionless
dispassionate you know activity you know
and um and so hopefully I'm I'm
stressing that point home in these macro
minutes uh I think there was another
part of the question um

coins so just one final thing I'll say
is um with going back to this this

valuation of about at a valuation of
about $500 billion which is on par with

um private company uh I'm pretty sure

and the AI race and SpaceX obviously one
of the most you know innovative and uh
and quite possibly could go down as one
of the most you know important companies
of all time in terms of all the
satellites they're putting up there in
space. You know, a company that is
basically being financially repressed in
buying tea bills having a valuation at
that level is either a sign of a the
peak the top is is is not is near or
it's a sign that global liquidity is not
only rising but it's accelerating in
terms of its growth rate which is
something our global liquidity model has
signaled for months. In our opinion, if
you look at the, you know, the the the
returns in unprofitable tech, uh you
look at the the valuation of a company
that's basically that's happy to be
financially repressed in a buying T
bills in an era where we're likely to
see structural gene change in the Fed
catalyze dribbly negative real uh floor
of fed funds rates in the coming, you
know, let's call it 18 24 months. If
we're, you know, there's enough money to
go around to value Tether like like Open
AI and and SpaceX then and and and and
and capitalize profitable unprofitable
tech companies to the tune of a 21% bull
markets is the end of July. Then to me,
what that signals is that liquidity in
conditions are actually improving
financial markets. Liquidity first dries
up at the fringes at the margins. the
more small cap you are, the more further
out on the risk spectrum you are uh in
terms of your asset class, the the more
it breaks down sooner when things are
about to go wrong because you need more
and more liquidity to support those
positions. And so once liquidity starts
to dry up, it it attacks those holdings
first and then over time mors back
towards the core of the financial system
which is you know US equities, US
treasuries, uh US dollar and those types
of assets. So the fact that we're doing
the exact opposite tells me that you
know our global liquidity model which
has been forecasting uh an acceleration
in global liquidity significant
acceleration in global liquidity of late
uh is uh is like is correct and likely
to continue to be correct. So these are
all dynamics I wouldn't fight uh you
know like I said earlier you know you
don't have to live in fear as an
investor in a raging bull market. You
know you can just trust that Kiss and
Dr. Mo are going to do what Kiss and Dr.
have always done which is you know chop
off the left tail of the distribution uh
and and help you know thousands of
investors around the world including
myself sleep comfortably at night. You
know you don't have to live in fear of
being wrong in your fundamental beliefs
or of me being wrong on my fundamental
beliefs. You know us at 42 macro you
know we could be dead wrong on paradigm
C starting right now. This could be the
absolute top tick of every market right
now. And guess what I don't care about

when they tell me to you know reduce
